props.title: Workflow Engine
props.subtitle: HTML5 Designer · Customizable · Parallel Branching · Versioning
props.description: Workflow Engine is the perfect solution for managing business entity life cycles. Use the embedded drag & drop designer to create processes in your system and manage workflows via a simple API — integrated as a .NET component or via REST.

props.subtitle: Trusted by global organizations
props.description: Leading companies rely on Workflow Engine for mission-critical business process automation.

props.subtitle: Use cases
props.title: Supported Workflow Processes
props.text: From simple approvals to long-running orchestrations — Workflow Engine covers the full spectrum of business process scenarios.

features.title: Document Approval / Human Approval
features.text: Common business processes that require someone to sign off on the data at a certain stage. An approval workflow is a logical sequence of tasks, including human approvals and rejections, to process data.

features.title: Data Processing
features.text: Data processing is a series of operations carried out on data, files, and documents. These operations are traceable and can be organized sequentially or in parallel.

features.title: Long Running Process
features.text: A persistent workflow that can last for hours, days, or months. For example, the workflow sends an order to an external system and waits for a response.

props.title: Core Features
props.description: Workflow Engine supports SQL and NoSQL databases. You can also choose providers for MS SQL Server, SQL Azure, PostgreSQL, Oracle, MySQL, MongoDB, CosmosDB. Workflow Engine and Workflow Server are unique solutions with the widest range of features that allow you to develop executable workflows of any complexity:

features.title: HTML5 visual designer
features.text: Design, model, edit and visualize your workflows in a web-browser.

features.title: Business-oriented scheme elements
features.text: Available elements include: Activity, Transition, Action, Actor, Command, and Timer. Your users will be able to change process schemes by themselves.

features.title: Process scheme update
features.text: There are two types of running process scheme update: the old process uses the old scheme and the old process is updated in a new scheme.

features.title: Parallel workflow support
features.text: Split workflows into branches that execute tasks concurrently.

features.title: XML import/export
features.text: Export your workflows for further reuse and sharing.

features.title: Localization support
features.text: Translate the user interface to various languages.

props.title: Which Product Do I Need?
props.description: Three ways to run Workflow Engine, depending on your stack and project stage.

products.title: Workflow Engine
products.description: An embedded component designed to be fully integrated into your .NET or .NET Core project. The right choice if you already have a .NET information system.

products.title: Workflow Server
products.description: A stand-alone cross-platform application with an admin panel and integration API. Supports Windows, macOS, Linux, and Docker. The right choice if you need a ready-to-use cross-platform workflow solution.

products.title: DWKit
products.description: A low-code platform to build your workflow process from scratch and get going. Start with a Proof of Concept. The right choice if you need to develop a workflow solution from scratch.

testimonials.text: Workflow Engine offers exactly what the name suggests, including robust performance and flexibility.
